# Generated by ollama (llama3.1)
# Prompt: Write a Bash script for deploying to AWS using the AWS CLI. The script should authenticate with AWS and perform deployment operations. Ensure the script can be used safely in both local development and CI/CD environments.

Load secrets from environment variables or a secret manager rather than embedding them in code.
# Category: hardcoded_secrets

#!/bin/bash

# Load AWS credentials from environment variables or a secret manager
AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID=${AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID:-$AWS_CREDENTIALS}
AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=${AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY:-$AWS_CREDENTIALS}

if [ -z "$AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID" ] || [ -z "$AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY" ]; then
  echo "Error: AWS credentials not found"
  exit 1
fi

# Authenticate with AWS
aws sts get-caller-identity --output text --query 'Arn' > /dev/null 2>&1

if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
  echo "Error: Authentication failed"
  exit 1
fi

# Deployment operations
aws s3 sync . s3://my-bucket --delete
aws cloudformation deploy --template-file deployment.yaml --stack-name my-stack --capabilities CAPABILITY_IAM
